overview.title: Overview
paragraphs: Linux Authentication Log integration uses the Auditbeat Auditd module to collect Linux Audit subsystem events. Through structured audit data, it helps you implement security compliance, detect intrusion behavior, and trace system calls and user operations.

items.title: User Operation Auditing
items.description: Record user logins, privilege escalation, su/sudo usage

items.title: System Call Tracking
items.description: Monitor sensitive system calls like execve, open, connect

items.title: Security Event Monitoring
items.description: Detect unauthorized access, permission changes, security policy modifications

items.title: Compliance Auditing
items.description: Meet PCI-DSS, HIPAA, SOX and other compliance requirements

fieldTables.title: Parsed Fields
fieldTables.description: Audit events will be parsed into the following structured fields:

tips.title: Root Privileges Required
tips.content: Auditbeat requires root privileges to read audit events. Ensure the service runs as root or with CAP_AUDIT_READ capability.
